WTI.data_anomaly: fast_info prev_close $104.85 (-7.6%) vs history prev_close $96.57 (+0.3%). Using fast_info as headline; history used for technicals only.

macro_summary: SPY closed up 0.8% at $686.10 and QQQ gained 0.8% to $617.39, with VIX easing to 19.12, reflecting modest risk-on sentiment even as the US-Iran Hormuz blockade entered a new phase on April 13. Per Argus Media, the US Navy blockade took effect at 10am ET Monday, targeting vessels transiting to or from Iranian ports — with approximately 36% of prior Hormuz tanker traffic linked to Iranian voyages. Iran reportedly signalled willingness to negotiate, per President Trump's comments to reporters, though no formal agreement has been reached and the Strait remains effectively closed. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement is scheduled for release at 8pm ET tonight — a HIGH-impact catalyst for the Singapore bank cluster that could shift rate expectations for the SGD-denominated lending environment.
commodities_note: WTI data shows a conflict: the fast_info source reports a headline price of $96.90 with a -7.58% daily change, while the historical data shows a prior close of $96.57 implying only a +0.3% move. This appears to be a data artifact — the 5-day context and news digest confirm WTI surged to approximately $104.91 (+8.64%) on April 13 as the blockade was confirmed. Use the $96-105 range as the operative band; the -7.58% headline figure is unreliable. Per Morningstar and Argus Media, WTI was trading above $100/bbl intraday on blockade confirmation, with analyst range now $100-135. Gold gained 1.04% to $4,791/oz, pushing further into record territory — no attractive entry exists on GLD with the ETF extended well above its 50-day average. The DXY fell 0.61% to 98.41 as flight-to-safety demand moderated slightly, and the 10-year US Treasury yield dipped to 4.30% — still above the 4.20% threshold where NIM pressure on Singapore banks would ease.

NVDA.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. NVDA sits 4.0% above its 50-day average of $182.01 at $189.31 with RSI at 62 — extended but not extreme. Invalidation is at the 50-day average ($182.01); a close below that level would confirm the rally has failed. R:R to nearest resistance at $194.67 is 0.7:1, which is unfavourable for a fresh entry at current levels — the upside to the first meaningful ceiling is only 2.8% versus 3.9% to invalidation. Volume is below average, which adds caution to the setup. Fundamentally, NVDA trades at 17x forward earnings — a 22.7% discount to the semis cluster median of 22x — with 73% revenue growth and a PEG of 0.77, making it the cheapest quality name on the watchlist relative to growth. The Blackwell Ultra ramp remains the load-bearing thesis leg (per NVDA Q4 FY2026 earnings), and the $78B Q1 FY2027 guidance implies the revenue ramp analysts modelled for Q3 is pulling forward. However, the fragility gate applies: the thesis rests primarily on Blackwell adoption continuity — max WATCH until a second independent leg (e.g. LITE co-packaged optics demand confirmation, or sustained multi-day volume on the current breakout) emerges. Bottom line: NVDA is the most attractively valued semi on the watchlist, but wait for a pullback to the $182-184 zone before entering — that's where the trade makes sense.

AMD.entry_block: BLOCKED: +18.0% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=7 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 71 (>=55).

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

AMD.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMD has surged 18% above its 50-day average of $209.24 with RSI at 70.8 — overbought and hard-blocked from entry. The pipeline has enforced a hard block at this extension level, and no trend exception applies with only 7 days above the SMA50 and the average itself declining. Per KeyBanc (Barchart, April 13), both Intel and AMD are expected to benefit from higher CPU demand — a supportive read-through, but AMD is already priced for near-perfection at these levels. The Meta GPU framework (per AMD IR, February 24, 2026) for MI450-based deployments remains the structural thesis, but this is a framework ceiling — not a firm purchase order — and first shipments aren't until H2 2026. At 22.8x forward earnings with a PEG of 0.73 and 34% revenue growth, the fundamental picture is attractive, but the technical setup is not. The R:R at current levels is 0.5:1 — with 15.2% downside to invalidation versus 7.7% upside to resistance. Bottom line: AMD has the right fundamental story but has run too far, too fast — wait for a pullback to the $193-209 support zone before reconsidering entry.

INTC.entry_block: BLOCKED: +37.1% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

INTC.sma50_warning: SMA50 is 27% below price — pullback to SMA50 would require a crash-level move. Use shorter-term support levels for entry guidance instead.
INTC.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Intel is 37% above its 50-day average of $47.54 — an extreme extension that triggers an absolute block on entry regardless of the catalyst. The Tesla chip deal, cited in 24/7 Wall St. (April 14), is generating significant momentum — nine straight gaining sessions — but the technical setup is uninvestable at current levels: RSI at 77.8, R:R at 0.2:1 (5.0% upside to $68.44 versus 27.1% downside to SMA50), and negative free cash flow yield of -1.38%. As noted in the standing thesis, the 18A foundry node has no confirmed external customers as of April 2026 — internal products only. The Tesla deal, if confirmed as an 18A manufacturing win rather than a chip design collaboration, would be the first genuine external foundry proof point and potentially thesis-changing — but the article does not clarify the nature of the deal, so it cannot be treated as foundry confirmation. Forward PE of 64x with negative revenue growth (-4.1%) and a 190.9% premium to the semis cluster median is an extremely stretched valuation. Shorter-term support around $55-58 is more relevant than the SMA50 for entry planning given the sma50 warning. Bottom line: INTC's story is improving but the stock has already priced in a turnaround that hasn't been proven — avoid entry here and wait for a meaningful pullback to the $55-60 zone.

ASML.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. ASML reports earnings tomorrow (April 15) — a binary event that makes any entry today inadvisable regardless of the technical setup. The stock is 7.7% above its 50-day average of $1,393.24 at $1,500.20, hard-blocked by the pipeline, with only 4 days above that average and RSI at 63. Per Investopedia (April 13), traders are anticipating the stock could reach record highs following results — but this represents positioning risk in both directions. The Hormuz crisis adds a secondary headwind: per the news digest, logistics cost inflation and supply chain rerouting pressure ASML's supply chain. At 33.2x forward earnings with a PEG of 2.43 and only 4.9% revenue growth, ASML carries a 50.9% premium to the semis cluster median — expensive for the growth on offer. Structural support sits at $1,283.60 (14.4% below), which is the level that would truly test whether the thesis holds. Bottom line: ASML is interesting on fundamentals, but tomorrow's earnings make entry today a binary bet — wait for the print and a potential pullback toward $1,400 before reconsidering.

MU.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.9% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=4 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 59 (>=55).

MU.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. MU is 5.9% above its 50-day average of $402.63 at $426.56 — just over the hard block threshold — with RSI at 58.7 and below-average volume. Per Barron's (April 13), KeyBanc views MU as having one of the best risk/reward setups into earnings season and projects another 40% gain — a bullish call from a sell-side firm that warrants noting but also appropriate scepticism. The forward PE of 4.3x versus a trailing PE of 20.1x implies a dramatic earnings inflection is expected; the PEG of 0.25 appears anomalous given the 4.3x forward PE — verify earnings growth estimate before citing as a primary valuation argument. Revenue growth of 196.3% and analyst consensus expecting 756% earnings growth validate the HBM demand cycle, which is driven by inference workloads (KV cache scaling) rather than training. FCF yield of 0.6% is low. Structural support sits at $357.52 (16.2% below). R:R at current levels is 0.5:1 — unfavourable for entry. Bottom line: MU has the fundamental tailwind but is technically extended — worth watching for a pullback toward $400-410 where the risk/reward improves meaningfully.

TSM.entry_block: BLOCKED: +5.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=4 (<30), RSI 62 (>=55).

TSM.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. TSMC reports earnings on April 16 — in two trading days — making any entry today a binary bet on the print. At 5.4% above its 50-day average of $350.55 at $369.57, the stock is hard-blocked, with RSI at 62 and 4 days above the average. Per Zacks (April 13), TSMC is one of three notable names on this week's reporting docket alongside Netflix and PepsiCo. Fundamentally, at 20x forward earnings with a PEG of 1.3 and 20.5% revenue growth, valuation is reasonable — a 9.1% discount to the semis cluster median. The CoWoS packaging bottleneck remains the structural thesis: capacity scaling from approximately 80,000 to 120-130,000 wafers per month by year-end, with NVDA holding more than half of allocated capacity. The Hormuz disruption adds logistics cost pressure as a secondary headwind. Structural support at $315.99 (14.5% below) is the meaningful downside reference if earnings disappoint. Bottom line: TSMC is the right thesis at the wrong moment — wait for the April 16 earnings print before considering any entry, with the $350-360 zone as the ideal re-entry level on a positive reaction.

AMZN.entry_block: BLOCKED: +12.4% above 50-day SMA (>5% hard block). Sustained trend exception not met: days_above_sma50=4 (<30), sma50 not rising, RSI 72 (>=55).

AMZN.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. AMZN has extended 12.4% above its 50-day average of $213.37 at $239.89 with RSI at 72.2 — overbought and hard-blocked. The stock has recovered sharply from recent lows, but the R:R at current levels is 0.3:1 (3.5% upside to nearest resistance at $248.36 versus 11.1% downside to SMA50 invalidation). Per Simply Wall St. (April 14), the Project Kuiper Delta Air Lines partnership marks Kuiper's first major commercial deployment — a forward revenue signal outside the core AWS/e-commerce business. The $200B AI capex plan remains the thesis centrepiece, but per the standing thesis, the key question is when capex translates to earnings — analyst consensus expects only 5% earnings growth despite strong revenue momentum. At 25.6x forward earnings with a PEG of 1.84 and 13.6% revenue growth, valuation is not stretched but not cheap either. Bottom line: AMZN has moved too far too fast — wait for a pullback to the $220-225 support zone, where the risk/reward would improve substantially, before reconsidering entry.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

GOOG.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at WATCH. Google is now essentially at its nearest resistance level of $319.23 — the pipeline has correctly flagged a tight upside situation. Nearest resistance at $319.23 (0.0% above) is within a single session's move; R:R uses $328.22 (2.8% above) as the meaningful ceiling. Invalidation sits at the 50-day average of $307.36, giving a ratio of 0.8:1 — unfavourable for a fresh entry here. RSI at 64.2 is neutral-bullish but not oversold. Below-average volume is a caveat. Per Insider Monkey (April 13), Google entered a long-term agreement with Broadcom (announced April 7) to develop and supply custom TPUs through 2031, alongside networking components — this validates the custom silicon thesis and reduces NVDA GPU dependence for Google's own workloads. The TPU v7 dominance narrative (per the catalyst file) has Meta renting capacity and a multi-year deal in place. At 23.8x forward earnings with a PEG of 1.86 and 18% revenue growth, Google is roughly in line with the BigTech cluster median. Earnings on April 30 will be the major thesis test — capex guidance will determine whether the $1.7T by 2030 hyperscaler build remains credible. Bottom line: Google is fairly priced for what it is, but the stock is pressing resistance right now — the better entry is a pullback to $307-312 ahead of April 30 earnings.

MSFT.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. MSFT is 2.1% below its 50-day average of $392.66 at $384.37 — technically on the wrong side of the average with a declining SMA50 pointing to sustained pressure. RSI at 51.5 is neutral, and volume was above average on the +3.4% session, which is encouraging — but one above-average session does not constitute a trend reversal. Invalidation at nearest support of $381.71 is only 0.7% below current price — a tight stop that normal intraday volatility could trigger. Use structural support at $356.28 (7.3% below) as the mental stop for position sizing. The R:R is mathematically distorted at 38.6:1 due to the near-zero denominator and is therefore not meaningful. Per Insider Monkey (April 14), BNP Paribas cut its Microsoft price target by $103 ahead of the Q3 earnings report on April 30 — a notable headwind. The standing thesis on OpenAI concentration risk remains relevant: approximately 45% of Microsoft's commercial backlog is tied to OpenAI, making non-OpenAI RPO growth the cleaner demand signal to watch. At 20.3x forward earnings with a PEG of 1.25 and 16.7% revenue growth, Microsoft actually trades at a 17.8% discount to the BigTech cluster median — the valuation is not the problem; the technical position and near-term earnings uncertainty are. Bottom line: MSFT is arguably the best-value name in BigTech right now, but it's below a declining average heading into a high-stakes earnings print — wait for the April 30 report before deciding whether to enter.

AVGO.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at HOLD. Broadcom has surged 16.1% above its 50-day average of $327.11 at $379.75 with RSI at 73.1 — overbought and hard-blocked. Nearest resistance at $384.93 (1.4% above) is tight upside; the pipeline uses $401.39 (5.7% above) as the meaningful ceiling, giving a R:R of 0.4:1 against 13.9% downside to SMA50 invalidation. Per the standing thesis, Broadcom is the dominant custom AI ASIC designer — Google TPU, Meta training chips, OpenAI/ByteDance inference — making it a direct competitive alternative to NVDA's merchant GPU model. The VMware acquisition adds software recurring revenue. At 21.2x forward earnings with a PEG of 0.83 and 29.5% revenue growth, the blended hardware-software valuation is compelling. The concern is purely technical: after a 20.8% 5-day surge, profit-taking risk is elevated. Bottom line: Broadcom has the right business model for the AI era, but it's too extended to enter here — wait for a pullback toward the $340-355 zone, where the setup would be much cleaner.

LITE.entry_block: BLOCKED: +30.3% above 50-day SMA (>20% extreme extension). No trend exception applies at this level — entry requires a meaningful pullback regardless of trend duration.

risk_reward.invalidation_reason: 50-day SMA -- break below confirms rally failure

LITE.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. LITE is 30.3% above its 50-day average of $668.67 at $871.18 — extreme extension that triggers an absolute block regardless of trend duration. Despite 201 consecutive days above the SMA50 with a rising average, the extreme extension rule is categorical. Per Insider Monkey (April 13), JPMorgan raised its price target to $950 from $565, citing improved forward visibility across growth drivers — a significant revision that validates the NVDA $2B strategic investment thesis (per NVIDIA's investor relations press release, March 2, 2026). However, the FCF yield is -0.03% (negative), forward PE of 55x represents a 150% premium to the semis cluster median, and the PEG of 0.15 appears anomalous given 55x forward PE — the earnings growth estimate underlying this should be verified before treating PEG as a primary argument. The NVDA co-packaged optics thesis remains intact: optical interconnects are the bandwidth bottleneck for GPU clusters beyond 100,000 GPUs, and this is a structural multi-year story. The S&P 500 inclusion buying (March 9, 2026) has long since exhausted. Structural support at $642.37 (26.3% below) is the meaningful downside reference. Bottom line: LITE has the right thesis and institutional backing, but at 30% above its 50-day average, there is no good entry — the risk/reward only becomes interesting on a pullback toward $700-750.

PLTR.entry_block: CAUTION: forward P/E of 71x (>40x). Requires explicit justification of why earnings growth supports this multiple — default to WATCH unless thesis is clearly articulated with specific catalysts and timeline.
PLTR.signal_rationale: Signal unchanged at CAUTION. PLTR is 8% below its 50-day average of $143.94 at $132.37 with RSI at 38.9 — technically broken but approaching an interesting zone. The pipeline's soft block on entry stems from the 71x forward PE, which requires explicit justification. Per the catalyst file, the $10B Army contract is an IDIQ framework ceiling — not firm revenue — and the $448M Navy ShipOS deal (per DoD press release) is the confirmed component. Rosenblatt raised its price target to $200 (per analyst notes). The 2026 revenue guide of $7.18-7.20B implies 61% year-over-year growth — exceptional, but the PEG of 2.53 and 71x forward PE (187.9% premium to the BigTech cluster median) remain extremely stretched. R:R at current levels is 0.3:1 — 1.5% upside to nearest resistance at $134.39 versus 4.6% to invalidation at support $126.30. RSI at 38.9 is approaching but not yet at the contrarian threshold of 38 — it misses the contrarian gate by 0.9 points. Support at $119.17 (10% below) is the structural backstop. Bottom line: PLTR's defense AI story is real, but 71x forward earnings prices in perfection — avoid entry here and revisit if RSI breaks below 35 and the stock tests the $119-126 support zone.

singapore.summary: All three names remain technically extended from entry: D05 at WATCH (2.2% above SMA50), U11 at WATCH (0.6% above), O39 hard-blocked at 5.8% above. The MAS Monetary Policy Statement tonight is the session-defining catalyst — any dovish signal could be the first credible break from the NIM compression narrative that has dominated for 35+ days. US10Y at 4.30% remains above the 4.20% reset threshold, but the direction is favourable (down 4.6 basis points).
singapore.key_development: MAS Monetary Policy Statement due tonight (8pm ET) — could shift SGD rate expectations and upgrade D05 or U11 if dovish.

semiconductors.summary: SOXX closed up 1.85% to $393.34, extending the week's recovery but with every watchlist semi still technically blocked from entry — NVDA at WATCH (4% above SMA50), MU/TSM/ASML/AVGO on CAUTION (all hard-blocked), AMD/INTC on HOLD (18% and 37% above SMA50 respectively). The cluster bifurcation between quality (NVDA, AVGO) and foundry plays (ASML, TSM, MU) is narrowing as both groups extend simultaneously. ASML earnings tomorrow and TSM earnings Wednesday are the twin catalysts that could reprice the entire cluster — strong prints with maintained guidance would validate the AI capex thesis despite Hormuz logistics headwinds.
semiconductors.thesis_status: Rally extended, binary earnings week ahead
semiconductors.key_development: ASML reports April 15 and TSM reports April 16 — back-to-back prints that will set the tone for the AI capex narrative heading into Q2.

big_tech.summary: The cluster is split between extended names (AMZN at 12.4% above SMA50, AVGO at 16.1% — both HOLD) and technically broken MSFT (2.1% below SMA50, CAUTION), with GOOG at WATCH pressing resistance. The dominant divergence is MSFT: despite trading at a 17.8% discount to cluster median forward PE, it's the only name below a declining 50-day average heading into three April 30 earnings (GOOG, MSFT, AMZN). The actionable cluster trade is patience — all four names have April 30 or May 1 earnings within 16-17 days, making fresh entries in any of them a pre-earnings positioning bet rather than a clean technical setup.
big_tech.thesis_status: Extended and overbought, cluster earnings window approaching
big_tech.key_development: GOOG, MSFT, and AMZN all report within 16-17 days — Q1 capex guidance will be the single most important data point for the hyperscaler AI buildout thesis.

active_risks: US Navy Hormuz blockade in effect as of April 13, targeting vessels to/from Iranian ports — Strait effectively closed with supply reopening timeline measured in weeks, per Argus Media
US-China tech restrictions and rare earths leverage escalating alongside Hormuz crisis — dual supply chain reshaping pressure on semiconductor exports
Fed maintaining hawkish rate pause with US10Y at 4.30% — oil shock threatening to re-ignite inflation and delay any rate cuts
MAS Monetary Policy Statement tonight — could shift SGD rate expectations and break the Singapore bank cluster NIM compression narrative
geopolitical.market_impact: The Hormuz blockade has driven WTI into the $100-105 range and pushed logistics costs higher, creating a secondary headwind for ASML and TSM supply chains that layers on top of the US-China tariff friction. PLTR remains the primary watchlist beneficiary of sustained geopolitical escalation, though the stock is technically broken at 8% below its 50-day average with a stretched 71x forward PE limiting fresh entry appeal.
geopolitical.new_since_yesterday: US Naval blockade formally took effect at 10am ET April 13, per Argus Media — Iran reportedly signalled willingness to negotiate per President Trump's comments, but no agreement was reached and the blockade remains active. OPEC kept its global supply and demand forecasts unchanged despite the disruption, per Argus Media's Monthly Oil Market Report.
geopolitical.portfolio_action: No new entries in energy-exposed or logistics-dependent names until Hormuz resolution becomes credible — selective accumulation in semis only on post-earnings pullbacks to SMA50 support.
